docs.title: Home
docs.text:
RAVE SPINXR

With the power of Extended Reality (XR) Become a DJ: ANYWHERE, ANYTIME, and mix on-the-go!

Import your own songs and record your sessions in .WAV format

Based off the famous Pioneer DDJ-FLX4

Watch the RaveSpin XR Trailer here!

docs.title: What is RaveSpin XR?
docs.text:
The idea behind Rave-Spin XR, is through the use of an XR headset, such as the Meta Quest 3S, give experienced DJs (Disk Jockeys) the opportunity to make and mix music, without the need for the usual physical equipment being present, thus allowing them to mix and play on-the-go.

With the included tooltips, beginner guides on YouTube and the Official RaveSpin XR website , and its inherent ease-of-use, Rave-Spin XR allows beginners to try out DJing for themselves and learn the various techniques and components without the required financial investment in the relevant hardware.

With the growing interest in electronic music and people learning to DJ and mix, this will allow people to try out the artform for themselves and gauge whether it’s a passion they would like to pursue.

docs.title: How does it work?
docs.text: The application is loaded onto the users’ headset. All audio processing, hand-tracking, rendering, File-IO, metadata extraction from songs, is all locally run on the users’ headset. There is explicitly no online requirement. Sessions may be recorded by the user, saving all output to the users’ headset, where it can be easily shared, or easily extracted via USB to the user’s computer using the included tools.

docs.title: Install
docs.text: RaveSpin XR can be installed to your headset by deploying the app straight to your headset.

docs.title: Prerequistites
docs.text:
• OpenJDK (as mentioned by Godot Docs)
  • Godot v4.6
  • ADB (Usally packaged with Android Studio )
  • Patience, (Lots of it)
  • Git

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Once you have Godot installed, we need to download RaveSpin XR Then we want to open Godot and import our project.

  1. Clone the RaveSpin XR Git Repo by running git clone https://github.com/marc-rene/RaveSpin.git in your terminal.

  2. Open Godot and Click "Import"

  3. Navigate to ../RaveSpin/Godot and click on project.godot

  4. If it's your first time running Godot, you may need to install the android build template. If you have this installed you can skip this step.

  5. Make sure in your Godot Editor Settings, that you have the Java SDK Path and the Android SDK Path filled in.

  6. In Godot, click on the Remote Deploy button in the toolbar. Your Headset should appear shortly if it's connected.

  7. After waiting a few seconds, the Headset should appear. Click on the Headset, or press Shift + F5

docs.text:

ROOT_MUSIC_DIR = "res://Music/Song Metadatas/" 🔗

Song metadata resource used by LibreBox and DJ_Controller. Stores music metadata plus stream/waveform references for built-in and user-imported tracks. Root directory for built-in song metadata resources.

USER_SONG_METADATA_DIR = "user://Music/" 🔗

User-imported songs (Add Track on device) save .tres here so they persist on Quest/Android.

docs.title: Property Descriptions
docs.text:
StringName Song_Title 🔗

Song title shown in UI.

Artist Main_Artist 🔗

Primary artist for this song.

Array[Artist] Guest_Artists 🔗

Optional list of featured artists.

Album Song_Album 🔗

Album resource for this song. Singles can still use a same-name album resource.

EGenre.E_ID3Genres[] Song_Genres 🔗

Genre IDs describing this song. Done in Ints, use Int_to_Id3Genre_str() to get the Genre Display

AudioStream Audio_File 🔗

Playable audio stream asset for this song.

String Audio_File_Path 🔗

For user-imported songs (Add Local): path to the file under user:// so it persists. When set, get_audio_stream() loads from this path if Audio_File is null.

m_scales_enum Track_Scale 🔗

EMusicKey Track_Key = <unknown> 🔗

Derived key object built from note + scale values.

bool Favourite 🔗
